5.1mdBook to Marque5.2MkDocs to Marque
Extra
6.Markdown Syntax7.FAQ8.Author

Type to search titles, headings, and page content.

Search is ready. Try a page title, a section heading, or a phrase from the content.

Markdown Basic Syntax

Here's the basic syntax of Markdown, which you can use in your .mq files for content formatting. Marque supports standard Markdown syntax, as well as some extensions for enhanced functionality.

Headings and paragraphs

Headings

Heading level 1

Heading level 2

Heading level 3

Heading level 4

Heading level 5
Heading level 6

Heading syntax

md
# Heading level 1
## Heading level 2
### Heading level 3
#### Heading level 4
##### Heading level 5
###### Heading level 6

Alternate headings

Heading level 1

Heading level 2

Alternate heading syntax

md
Heading level 1
===============

Heading level 2
---------------

Paragraphs

This is a paragraph.

This is another paragraph.

First line.
Second line.

Paragraph and line break syntax

md
This is a paragraph.

This is another paragraph.

First line.
Second line.

Emphasis and inline syntax

Bold, italic, and both

  • **bold text** or __bold text__ -> bold text
  • *italic text* or _italic text_ -> italic text
  • ***bold and italic*** -> bold and italic

Inline code and escaping

  • Use `code` like this. -> Use code like this.
  • \* Not italic \* -> * Not italic *

Blockquotes and lists

Blockquotes

Single-line blockquote syntax can stay inline: > This is a blockquote.

This is a blockquote.

Nested blockquote syntax:

md
> First level
>
> > Second level

First level

Second level

Lists

Ordered list syntax:

md
1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item
  1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Third item

Unordered list syntax:

md
- First item
- Second item
- Third item
  • First item
  • Second item
  • Third item

Nested list syntax:

md
- Item
  - Subitem
  • Item
    • Subitem

Code, links, media, and rules

Code

Inline code: Use `code` like this.

Use code like this.

Code block syntax:

md
```text
code block
```
text
code block

Links and images

Link syntax:
[Google](https://www.google.com) -> Google

Image syntax:

md
![Alt text](https://placehold.co/600x400)

Horizontal rules

Any of these will render a horizontal rule:

  • ---
  • ***
  • ___

Best Practices

  • Leave blank lines between elements
  • Don't indent paragraphs
  • Use consistent list markers
  • Prefer * for emphasis compatibility

Built with Marque
